mission.title: argo: Ability to generate Account token for SSO logged user
mission.description: Ability to generate Account token for SSO logged user. Requested by 63+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
helm list -n argo 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo installation.

steps.title: Review argo configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
kubectl get configmap -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo
```
it is impossible to generate an account token for a SSO  authenticated user  using  argocd CLI or through the GUI.
 
```
$ argocd login argo.*** --sso --grpc-web
Opening browser for authentication
Performing authori

steps.title: Apply the fix for Ability to generate Account token for SSO logged user
steps.description:
Add new session-token subcommand to argocd account that displays the current authentication token for both local and SSO users.

Features:
- Shows current session token (works with both local and SSO authentication)
- Auto-refreshes expired SSO tokens using refresh token when --refresh flag is
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: argocd-cm
data:
  # This is a test account used for development
  accounts.test: apiKey
  accounts.test.enabled: "true"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: argocd-rbac-cm
data:
  policy.test.csv: |
    p, test, applications, get, */*, allow
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
kubectl get events -n argo --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Ability to generate Account token for SSO logged user" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Add new session-token subcommand to argocd account that displays the current authentication token for both local and SSO users.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo installed or the issue environment reproducible.
